original_question:
Two swimmers, at opposite ends of a 90-foot pool, start to swim the length of the pool, one at the rate of 3 feet per second, the other at 2 feet per second. They swim back and forth for 12 minutes. Find the number of times they pass each other.

Present the answer in LaTex format: \boxed{Your answer}

similar_questions.question: Two runners, at opposite ends of a 120-meter track, start to run the length of the track, one at the rate of 4 meters per second, the other at 3 meters per second. They run back and forth for 10 minutes. Find the number of times they pass each other.

Two cyclists, at opposite ends of a 150-meter cycling path, start to cycle the length of the path, one at the rate of 5 meters per second, the other at 2.5 meters per second. They cycle back and forth for 15 minutes. Find the number of times they pass each other.

Two friends, at opposite ends of a 200-foot basketball court, start to run the length of the court, one at the rate of 6 feet per second, the other at 4 feet per second. They run back and forth for 8 minutes. Find the number of times they pass each other.

Two kayakers, at opposite ends of a 75-meter lake, start to paddle the length of the lake, one at the rate of 3.5 meters per second, the other at 1.5 meters per second. They paddle back and forth for 20 minutes. Find the number of times they pass each other.

Two joggers, at opposite ends of a 60-yard running track, start to jog the length of the track, one at the rate of 2 yards per second, the other at 1 yard per second. They jog back and forth for 5 minutes. Find the number of times they pass each other.
